Title: A chromosome-scale Rhubarb (Rheum tanguticum) genome assembly provides insights into the evolution of anthraquinone biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate β-glucosidase positively associated with anthraquinone accumulation in roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtaG0022724.1 encodes a putative BGL/GH1 β-glucosidase. 2. It is significantly more highly expressed in roots than in other tissues. 3. It clusters in the anthraquinone-correlated turquoise co-expression module, suggesting involvement in anthraquinone biosynthesis or tailoring.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome profiling across eight tissues; differential expression analysis; WGCNA co-expression with anthraquinone content; phylogenetic family analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root compared with other tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in root compared with other tissues
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/annotation; transcriptomics (NGDC BioProject PRJCA009275); metabolite profiling of five anthraquinones
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate tailoring gene for engineering anthraquinone composition in rhubarb after functional validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function remains candidate-level only; no direct enzymatic assay or transgenic validation reported
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactivity including laxative and other pharmacological effects

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate β-glucosidase positively associated with anthraquinone accumulation in roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtaG0009186.1 encodes a putative BGL/GH1 β-glucosidase. 2. It is significantly more highly expressed in roots than in other tissues. 3. It clusters in the anthraquinone-associated turquoise module, suggesting a role in anthraquinone biosynthesis or modification.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome profiling across eight tissues; differential expression analysis; WGCNA co-expression with anthraquinone content; phylogenetic family analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root compared with other tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in root compared with other tissues
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/annotation; transcriptomics (NGDC BioProject PRJCA009275); metabolite profiling of five anthraquinones
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate tailoring gene for anthraquinone metabolic engineering after validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate gene only; direct biochemical function in anthraquinone metabolism not proven
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactivity including laxative and other pharmacological effects

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthraquinone biosynthesis via the plant polyketide pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Anthraquinone scaffold formation / octaketide synthesis step
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: chalcone synthase-like type III polyketide synthase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; CHS family; PF02797 and PF00195
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively catalyzes successive decarboxylative condensations of malonyl-CoA to generate a linear octaketide precursor for anthraquinone scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: linear octaketide precursor / anthraquinone scaffold precursor
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the scaffold precursor of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Candidate key control point; not directly established biochemically
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong positive association with anthraquinone biosynthesis in roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among 28 CHS/CHS-L genes, RtaG0007463.1 showed the highest root expression. 2. It was identified as a hub gene with high connectivity in an anthraquinone-associated co-expression module. 3. Its expression pattern strongly implicates it as a core candidate for anthraquinone biosynthesis in rhubarb roots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide family identification; tissue transcriptomics; differential expression; WGCNA module membership and hub-gene analysis; metabolite correlation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expression in root among CHS genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest expression in root among CHS genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison across eight tissues under normal growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Root-expressed MYB and bHLH transcription factors in the turquoise module
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/annotation; transcriptomics (NGDC BioProject PRJCA009275); targeted metabolomics of aloe-emodin, rhein, chrysophanol, physcion, and emodin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Priority enzyme candidate for functional testing and engineering of anthraquinone production in rhubarb or heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is correlative and candidate-based; direct enzyme assay was not performed
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactivity including laxative, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, anticancer, and antimicrobial activities

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative oxidative tailoring reaction(s) in anthraquinone precursor or derivative biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product precursors or derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate root-enriched CYP450 associated with elevated anthraquinone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RtaG0030644.1 is one of four CYP450 hub genes in the turquoise module. 2. It is highly expressed in roots where anthraquinones accumulate. 3. It belongs to an expanded CYP450 family and is proposed as a candidate anthraquinone tailoring gene.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide CYP450 identification; tissue transcriptomics; differential expression; WGCNA hub-gene analysis; phylogenetic classification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root compared with other tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in root compared with other tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison across eight tissues under normal growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/annotation; transcriptomics (NGDC BioProject PRJCA009275); targeted metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate oxidative tailoring enzyme for modifying anthraquinone profiles after validation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact CYP subfamily, substrate, and reaction were not assigned; candidate only
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactivity including laxative and other pharmacological effects

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Name: Root-expressed MYB transcription factors interacting with RtaG0007463.1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthraquinone biosynthesis candidate regulation via CHS-associated module
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: RtaG0007463.1 and co-expressed anthraquinone biosynthetic candidates
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with CHS gene co-expression; exact direction not established
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate regulators in the anthraquinone-correlated turquoise module; two MYBs were hub genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putatively promote root anthraquinone biosynthesis through association with CHS expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Twelve MYB genes were clustered in the turquoise module associated with anthraquinone content. 2. Two MYBs were hub genes. 3. These TFs interacted with the CHS gene RtaG0007463.1, suggesting regulatory roles in anthraquinone biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome profiling; WGCNA co-expression network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-specific or root-enriched within turquoise module
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-specific or root-enriched within turquoise module
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulators for future manipulation of anthraquinone accumulation after gene-level validation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific MYB gene IDs were not provided in the text excerpt; regulatory role is inferred from co-expression rather than direct functional assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactivity including laxative and other pharmacological effects

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Name: Root-expressed bHLH transcription factors interacting with RtaG0007463.1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthraquinone biosynthesis candidate regulation via CHS-associated module
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: RtaG0007463.1 and co-expressed anthraquinone biosynthetic candidates
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with CHS gene co-expression; exact direction not established
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Seven bHLH genes in the turquoise module were linked to metabolite biosynthesis and CHS interaction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putatively promote or coordinate root anthraquinone biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seven bHLH genes were present in the turquoise module correlated with anthraquinone accumulation. 2. These bHLHs are known secondary-metabolism regulators and interacted with RtaG0007463.1 in the co-expression network. 3. They are candidate regulators of anthraquinone biosynthesis in rhubarb root.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome profiling; WGCNA co-expression network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-specific or root-enriched within turquoise module
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-specific or root-enriched within turquoise module
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream regulators for future breeding or synthetic biology after validation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific bHLH gene IDs were not provided in the text excerpt; evidence is network-based only
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactivity including laxative and other pharmacological effects
